data.title: Binance to Host Quiz Challenge with $200 Prize Pool
data.description:
Binance announced on X that it will host an interactive quiz challenge as part of its educational series, offering participants a chance to win a share of a $200 prize pool. The event, titled 'Quiz Challenge EP14,' invites all users to engage in a quick question-and-answer session, with rewards awaiting the top 20 participants.

The live session is scheduled to take place on April 16 at 20:00 (HKT), featuring host Soda. Participants can look forward to an engaging experience, with the opportunity to earn rewards based on their knowledge and interaction during the event. Additionally, the live stream will feature a special 'Red Packet' giveaway, where participants can trigger a prize ranging from $100 to $200 by reaching certain engagement and interaction milestones during the broadcast.

This initiative is part of Binance's ongoing efforts to enhance user engagement through educational and interactive content. By participating in the quiz challenge, users can not only test their knowledge but also potentially earn rewards, making it an attractive opportunity for both new and experienced users. The event underscores Binance's commitment to fostering a dynamic and interactive community, encouraging users to actively participate and benefit from their involvement in the platform's activities.

data.title: U.S. Cryptocurrency Market Structure Act Nears Final Negotiation Stage
data.description:
On April 16, analysts from JPMorgan indicated that negotiations over the U.S. Cryptocurrency Market Structure Act, known as the CLARITY Act, are approaching their final phase. According to BlockBeats, the discussions have narrowed down to a few remaining contentious issues, with stablecoin reward topics reportedly progressing well. Despite banks expressing concerns about stablecoins offering deposit-like returns, there is a bipartisan compromise emerging. JPMorgan acknowledges that while no legislation is perfect, the act's passage would provide crucial regulatory clarity for integrating digital assets into the U.S. financial system.

Currently, the Cryptocurrency Market Structure Act is in advanced negotiation stages within the U.S. Senate. Senate staff have noted that the draft is "very close" to resolution, although the final text has not been released, nor has a formal vote been scheduled. The remaining major disagreements focus on stablecoin rewards, DeFi regulation, and token classification. Although optimism is rising, the act faces potential delays due to the 2026 midterm elections, which could introduce a more uncertain political environment. If passed, the act would delineate regulatory authority between the SEC and CFTC, establishing a long-term regulatory framework for stablecoins, DeFi, and the broader crypto industry.

data.title: Crypto Fear & Greed Index Shows Neutral Sentiment
data.description: CoinMarketCap's Crypto Fear & Greed Index has risen to 55, indicating a neutral sentiment in the market. According to NS3.AI, this marks a two-point increase from the previous day, reflecting a stable outlook among investors.

data.title: TRUMP Token Plummets 96% from All-Time High
data.description: TRUMP token has experienced a significant decline, dropping 96% from its all-time high, as highlighted by Crypto Patel. According to NS3.AI, Crypto Patel noted that an investment of $10,000 made on January 19, 2025, would now be valued at just $351. CoinCodex has projected a further potential decrease, estimating the token's value could fall to $2.23 by the end of next month.

data.title: Cobo releases its payment incentive settlement layer ACL, open to public blockchains, stablecoin issuers, and payment institutions.
data.description: Cobo, a provider of digital asset custody and wallet solutions, has launched its Adoption Clearing Layer (ACL), open to public blockchains, stablecoin issuers, and payment institutions. The company aims to collaborate with more ecosystem partners to build a stablecoin payment network based on real transactions. ACL provides multi-chain payment routing and incentive settlement capabilities, directly linking ecosystem incentive budgets to real payment transactions, forming a transaction-centric growth loop. Initial ACL partners include Aptos and Morph, who can earn up to 15bps of rebates per transaction through ACL—helping ecosystem partners increase real on-chain transaction volume and providing payment institutions with a sustainable revenue stream.

data.title: Cobo Launches Payment Incentive Settlement Layer for Stablecoin Networks
data.description:
Digital asset custody and wallet solutions provider Cobo has introduced the Adoption Clearing Layer (ACL), aimed at public chains, stablecoin issuers, and payment institutions. According to Odaily, the initiative seeks to collaborate with more ecosystem partners to build a stablecoin payment network based on real transactions.

ACL offers multi-chain payment routing and incentive settlement capabilities, linking ecosystem incentive budgets directly to actual payment transactions. This creates a growth loop centered on transactions. Initial ACL partners include Aptos and Morph, with transactions through ACL eligible for a rebate of up to 15 basis points per transaction. This approach not only helps ecosystem partners increase genuine on-chain transaction volumes but also provides payment institutions with a sustainable revenue source.

data.title: Blackdove Ends Support for Foundation, Returns Control to Founder
data.description: Blackdove has announced the cessation of its support for Foundation, an Ethereum-based NFT platform. According to NS3.AI, operational control is being returned to the platform's founder, Kayvon. Kayvon and the original team are regrouping to continue their efforts in supporting the community.

data.title: Upbit adds SPURS to its trading alert list
data.description: Upbit has added SPURS to its transaction alert list.

data.title: Hong Kong Stock Changguang Chenxin Sees Significant Rise in Grey Market
data.description: Changguang Chenxin, listed under the ticker 03277.HK, experienced a substantial increase in the grey market, with shares surging over 68% at one point. According to Jin10, the stock is currently up by 65%.

data.title: RootData Urges Web3 Projects to Enhance Transparency
data.description:
Web3 asset data platform RootData has issued a transparency alert on Twitter (X), highlighting missing information from several projects, including LeverUp, Flash.Trade, PRDT, f(x) Protocol, and Tectonic. According to ChainCatcher, RootData is calling for these projects to submit or update their data on the platform to improve their transparency scores. RootData has committed to ongoing monitoring and exposure of projects lacking essential information.

The transparency score provided by RootData is designed to assess the completeness and timeliness of project disclosures, urging project teams to claim their projects promptly. The scoring system ranges from A to F, with lower scores indicating less comprehensive information disclosure and higher risk of misconduct, warranting increased caution from investors.

data.title: Risk appetite recovery drives capital recovery; Gate.com continues to optimize its multi-asset trading structure.
data.description: With easing geopolitical tensions and reduced inflationary pressures, market risk appetite has significantly recovered over the past week, leading to a decline in oil prices and a decrease in the VIX. BTC is currently fluctuating around $75,000. In terms of funding, institutional buying has regained dominance, with both the BTC ETF and ETH ETF recording net inflows. Regarding trading structure, funds are concentrating on highly liquid assets and leading platforms, with active trading in macro-volatile assets such as crude oil, and stablecoins and the DeFi ecosystem showing simultaneous recovery. Against this backdrop, Gate's institutional trading performance continues to improve, outperforming the market in both spot and futures markets, and maintaining its industry-leading position in derivatives. With the iteration of market-making fees and assessment mechanisms, the activity of mid-tier clients has increased, further improving the trading structure. CrossEx trading volume and fund accumulation have reached new highs, and it is accelerating collaboration with asset management and OTC loans. In terms of treasury operations, demand for mainstream assets such as ETH and USDT has rebounded, and AI customer service is gradually being implemented to improve institutional service efficiency. Meanwhile, Gate's multi-asset trading system continues to meet institutional demand. The platform covers multiple asset classes including metals, stocks, indices, forex, and commodities, with related derivatives trading remaining active. By combining the SuperLink architecture with cross-venue fund transfer capabilities, Gate continues to provide institutions with more flexible trading and risk management tools.

data.title: USD/JPY Exchange Rate Surpasses 159
data.description: The USD/JPY exchange rate has surpassed 159, marking a daily increase of 0.03%. According to ChainCatcher, this development reflects ongoing fluctuations in the currency market. The movement in the exchange rate is being closely monitored by investors and analysts as they assess its potential impact on global financial markets.

data.title: A senior Iranian official said that the chances of extending the US-Iran ceasefire and holding a second round of talks are greater after a visit by Pakistani military officials.
data.description: A senior Iranian official stated that the prospects for extending the US-Iran ceasefire and holding a second round of negotiations are greater following the visit of Pakistani military officials. Fundamental differences remain on the nuclear issue. (Jinshi)

data.title: Shinhan Financial Group to Integrate Crypto Wallet in New App
data.description: Shinhan Financial Group is set to incorporate a cryptocurrency wallet into its upcoming Shinhan SuperSOL application. According to NS3.AI, the group is also planning to trial a deposit token system in conjunction with the app.

data.title: Bitcoin Miner Reserves Decline by 61,000 BTC as Major Sellers Identified
data.description: Bitcoin miner reserves have decreased by approximately 61,000 BTC since the onset of the current cycle, according to NS3.AI. The reserves have dropped from around 1.862 million BTC to 1.801 million BTC. CryptoQuant has identified Riot Platforms, MARA Holdings, and Core Scientific as significant sellers contributing to this decline.

data.title: AI Demands New Strategies from CEOs
data.description: CEOs must abandon traditional business practices to adapt to the rapid changes brought by AI. According to PANews, leaders are urged to embrace new rules and a faster pace to effectively manage the impact of artificial intelligence on their industries.

data.title: Claw Wallet Enhances Security with GoPlus Partnership
data.description:
Web3 wallet infrastructure Claw Wallet has announced a strategic partnership with GoPlus. According to ChainCatcher, Claw Wallet has integrated GoPlus's SafuSkill Launchpad, which supports the assetization and tokenization of AI skills, enabling developers to earn ongoing on-chain revenue through GitHub verification.

Additionally, Claw Wallet has incorporated GoPlus's AgentGuard security scanning technology. This system provides real-time monitoring and risk visualization of AI Agent code, sensitive data, and operating environments through a multidimensional scoring system. The collaboration aims to enhance the security of AI Agent wallets and improve their ecosystem incentive mechanisms.

data.title: Italy's March Harmonized CPI Finalized at 1.6%
data.description: Italy's harmonized consumer price index (CPI) for March was finalized at an annual rate of 1.6%, according to Jin10. This figure slightly exceeded the anticipated rate of 1.5% and matched the previous month's rate of 1.50%.

data.title: Switzerland to Tighten Foreign Real Estate Purchase Restrictions Amid Housing Shortages
data.description: Switzerland is set to implement stricter regulations on foreign real estate purchases as the country faces increasing housing shortages. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's intention to address the growing demand for housing by limiting foreign investments in local properties. The move comes as Switzerland experiences a surge in housing demand, exacerbated by limited availability and rising prices. The proposed restrictions aim to prioritize local buyers and alleviate the pressure on the housing market. Authorities are expected to outline specific measures in the coming months, focusing on curbing foreign ownership to ensure more properties are accessible to Swiss residents. The decision reflects broader concerns about housing affordability and availability, which have become pressing issues in Switzerland's urban areas. As the government seeks to balance foreign investment with domestic needs, the new regulations are anticipated to impact the real estate market significantly.

data.title: South Korean President to Attend European-Led Summit on Strait of Hormuz Security
data.description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung is expected to participate in a European-led summit focused on securing shipping routes through the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg posted on X that this summit comes ahead of President Lee's planned visits to India and Vietnam. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical maritime passage, and discussions at the summit will likely address strategies to ensure safe navigation and stability in the region. President Lee's involvement underscores South Korea's commitment to international cooperation on maritime security issues.
